mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or remains flat after a prior peak, discount rates ease and equity valuation multiples expand, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal fails if rate cuts coincide with recessionary labor deterioration that overwhelms valuation support.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX forward returns are not positive more often than not during months when FEDFUNDS is declining, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend signals weakening household income and risk of profit disappointment, so S&P 500 returns turn negative over the next month. The effect is strongest when PAYEMS also softens, confirming labor-market broadening rather than a temporary participation shift.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE rises are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the signal loses significance once PYEMS is stable.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized improvement in RSAFS and GDPC1 indicates broad final-demand acceleration that supports revenue growth and earnings revisions, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The mechanism fails when the improvement is driven only by inventory rebuilding rather than true demand.
failure_condition: Reject if joint improvements in RSAFS and GDPC1 do not outperform either series alone, or if 60-day forward returns are not positive in expansionary observations.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates and FEDFUNDS remains above neutral, the market anticipates restrictive policy persistence and lower equity multiples, so S&P 500 returns are negative over the next 20 trading days. The effect fails if inflation rises while the Fed is clearly easing.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3m annualized inflation rises above its 12m average while FEDFUNDS > 4.5%
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction fails to produce negative 20-day SPX returns in triggered periods, or if the sign reverses when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO with stable or falling CPIAUCSL signals improving supply-side productivity and margin relief, which supports equity returns over the next month. The signal fails when output gains are accompanied by accelerating inflation, which implies demand overheating instead of supply improvement.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with rising INDPRO and non-accelerating CPIAUCSL do not show positive excess 20-day SPX returns relative to baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in PAYEMS reflects labor-market contraction and imminent earnings downgrades, so S&P 500 returns are negative over the next week. The mechanism is falsified if payroll declines are quickly reversed or occur without any market drawdown.
failure_condition: Reject if monthly payroll drops larger than 150k are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in most instances, or if the edge vanishes after revision-adjustment.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST rises from depressed levels, it signals renewed housing demand and an easing wealth effect channel that supports broader consumption and equity returns over the next quarter. The effect fails if higher housing starts occur while unemployment is rising, implying cyclical distress rather than demand strength.
failure_condition: Reject if post-threshold HOUST gains do not precede positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the effect disappears when UNRATE is falling.

mechanism_narrative: A broad sales slowdown in RSAFS combined with weaker PAYEMS signals impending consumption softening and earnings pressure, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal fails when sales weaken but payrolls remain strong, indicating temporary inventory or pricing effects instead of real demand loss.
failure_condition: Reject if joint weakness in RSAFS and PAYEMS does not correspond to negative 20-day SPX returns, or if the interaction has no incremental power over single-variable effects.
